title: Video-Model-based Single-View Scene Splitting and Reconstruction
teaser: Use a video generation model to isolate individual objects in a single-view scene, complete their hidden geometry, then re-assemble them into a full reconstructed 3D scene.
abstract: Video generation models are powerful for their temporal consistency, and with the right prompting or fine-tuning their spatial consistency can be exploited as well. This project targets a pipeline that, given a single image of a scene, prompts a video model to generate a clip in which one object is isolated and revealed from multiple viewpoints, exposing previously occluded geometry. The isolated views are fed to a 3D generation method (e.g., TRELLIS.2) to reconstruct the object, and a placement method positions each reconstructed object back into a coherent global scene. The student will manage the accumulated error across the prompting, reconstruction, and placement stages — the dominant engineering challenge.
milestones: Successfully prompt or fine-tune a video model (e.g., Wan) to isolate single objects from a scene image and produce multi-view-consistent clips.
Plug the resulting clips into a 3D reconstruction method (TRELLIS.2 or similar) to recover per-object geometry.
Develop a placement method that returns the reconstructed objects to their correct spatial locations in the original scene.

references.label: TRELLIS.2 — Native and Compact Structured Latents for 3D Generation
